gate.read: This decomposition is for the best measured rocWMMA selected-tile source-boundary path, not a promoted candidate.

measurements.name: mode0_score_only
measurements.scope: QK plus dO.V score-page production, softmax row terms, checksum sink

measurements.name: mode1_contribution_sink
measurements.scope: mode0 plus D16 contribution MFMA sweep, checksum sink

measurements.name: mode2_dkv_only
measurements.scope: mode1 plus group-aware global dKV atomics, no dQ

measurements.name: mode2_dq_dkv
measurements.scope: mode2 plus selected-tile dQ MFMA partial atomics

why: The score-page producer alone is 21.46 ms, already 1.93x slower than the 11.13 ms full sparse-attention backward gate.
The contribution sweep adds another 20.14 ms before any global writes.
The dKV global-write stage adds 11.67 ms and dQ partial accumulation adds 10.42 ms, but these are not the first wall for this branch.
Therefore, optimizing only the dKV epilogue or final dQ atomics cannot materialize a competitive full sparse-attention backward kernel from this rocWMMA selected-tile layout.

keep: source-boundary inputs: q, dO, output, lse, compressed KV, LI route
no materialized selected K/V, probability slab, or scaled-dP slab
change: Replace this rocWMMA score-page producer with a SGLang/Triton-like query-major online-softmax schedule or a CK block-attention schedule.
Keep Q/dO and selected KV reuse efficient first; only then add local dQ accumulation and group-aware dKV reduction.
Treat dKV epilogue work as secondary until the score/contribution producer is below the 8-11 ms band.
next_kernel_contract.candidate_next_branch: CK/HIP block-attention-style producer: token/head/query-major score and dO.V recompute with online row terms, then local dQ accumulation and compact/group-aware dKV partial rows. The first gate should be producer-only latency below the 11.13 ms full-boundary reference before adding final writes.

correctness.s4096_runs: timing-only decomposition; correctness skipped because the branch is latency-rejected
correctness.reference_oracle: reference_dq/reference_dkv in probe_sparse_mla_source_boundary_hip_coop.py remains wired for future correctness gates
